notes: full playthrough.  Noticed some minor issues.  In one place the scene was very dark as if gamma was set wrong.  After dying and continuing the scene was normal.  I saw a couple of places where the z-ordering was off (I could see textures that should be hidden).  Audio was very echoey (cave like) in a few locations (not sure if this is design intent or a bug)

title: Wolfenstein: The New Order

notes: Works right out of the box. No cloud saves, so I had to copy saves over manually, after some googling. Performance is a solid 60fps on the Ultra preset. It performed worse on Windows, and required tweaking to improve framerate. I have to point out, though, that it runs on an SSD in Linux, and an HDD in Windows, so if there's any major streaming from disk happening, that could account for the poorer performance in Windows. Regardless, I'll be completing the game in Linux.
title: Wolfenstein: The New Order

notes:
The game works perfectly out-of-the-box, I have played the entire game with proton without any problem, it has cloud saves and runs perfectly on the same driver configuration in Arch, Fedora 30 and Debian 10, I haven't encountered any performance issues with the game.
I'm using official Nvidia Optimus method to manage Optimus support, not tested with bumblebee and primus_vk or nvidia-prime.
title: Wolfenstein: The New Order

notes: Ran great out of the box. Installed Id5 Tweaker to remove 60 fps cap (follow README.MD directions then set launch options to: WINEDLLOVERRIDES="dinput8=n,b" %command%) and getting 100+ FPS on Ultra settings @ native res throughout.
title: Wolfenstein: The New Order
